what_it_does: A versioned JSON ledger that defines the stable visual-design substrate for an application: colors, typography, spacing, radii, shadows, chrome/texture, motion timings, dials, layout rules, content budgets, and usage constraints, with inline provenance notes.
observations: Meta declares version stable-1 and identifies the file as the source of truth for the stable token layer, edited by the token-playground.
Color section includes surfaces, accents, inks, an ordered warm-metal axis ramp, semantic colors, scaffold colors, a warm-pole gradient ramp, and role hues marked UNSAMPLED.
type_hues maps file extensions to palette categories (visual, docs, code, data, web) with folder/neutral/ghost fallback tokens.
warmth_anchors define cool and warm surface endpoints for a 0-100 warmth dial.
Type section defines font families, numeric scale, weights, and a role table binding scale/weight/color/one_line/alignment.
space, radius, shadow, chrome, texture, and motion_ms provide numeric and string tokens.
dials enumerates warmth, register, density, and modality axes.
gold_budget encodes rules limiting gold usage rather than just color values.
layout and content_budget encode responsive and overflow policies as tokens.
Many values are accompanied by _note fields explaining provenance, decisions, and estimated status.
standouts: gold_budget treats a color as a scarce resource with per-view caps and allowed treatments.
axis_ramp is explicitly an ordered OKLCH-interpolable scale for grammar-3 ordinal coloring.
type_hues uses a registry-driven category-to-palette mapping for graph file-type coloring.
content_budget overflow policy is a long procedural rule: reduce-density-then-stack-then-scroll-internally-never-clip-mid-sentence.
warmth_anchors separate cool and warm surface endpoints at near-equal lightness.
conventions: Nested JSON namespaces group related tokens (color, type, space, shadow, etc.).
Inline commentary is stored in _note string fields.
Cross-token references use symbolic names (e.g., colour: "charcoal") rather than raw values.
Provenance strings include dates, deck slide references, and decision owners.
Arrays are used for ordered ramps and enum-like dial values.
concerns: role_hues.values is explicitly UNSAMPLED and is a placeholder.
type.family.script_accent is ESTIMATED with the face unidentified.
chrome.glass and chrome.frost values are estimated, not pixel-sampled.
layout section is marked interim and intended for phone.css @media consumption before a future device-axis resolver.
Several notes call for future confirmation against real product chrome or captured product samples.
notes: This is the stable design-token source of truth: a comprehensive, namespaced token dictionary with extensive provenance and usage rules, though several sections remain estimated or unsampled.
questions: Which build-time or runtime layer resolves these JSON tokens into CSS custom properties or component variables?
How is the warmth dial interpolation implemented in code — OKLCH or RGB fallback?
What is the exact script accent font face intended for human-warmth moments?
How and when will role_hues values be captured from the product?
How are type_hue palette names such as cool-mint and soft-denim mapped to actual color values?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the canonical stable token layer and source of truth; the content matches that purpose, but it also contains interim/estimated/unsampled sections rather than being fully locked.
apparent_themes: Warm-metal brand identity with controlled, rare use of gold.
Semantic rather than decorative color application.
Ordered ramps and interpolation for systematic color scaling.
Adaptive UI via dials and responsive layout rules.
Inline provenance and decision audit trail as part of the token contract.
intention_signals: To establish a single inspectable contract between design intent and implementation, where tokens carry not only values but also rules, provenance, and constraints so generated UI can be audited and kept consistent.
novelty: The file elevates a design-token file into a ledger by combining traditional visual primitives with behavioral rules (gold_budget, content_budget overflow policy, dials) and inline provenance/commentary.
contribution: Provides a stable, namespaced vocabulary of visual primitives together with usage constraints and provenance metadata; a fused system would keep the token names, values, and rule annotations as a reference substrate.
summary_for_embedding: A versioned JSON design-token ledger defining stable colors, typography, spacing, shadows, motion, layout, and content rules for a warm-metal-branded application, annotated with provenance and usage constraints.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the stable source-of-truth design-token layer that records not only visual values but also the rules and provenance needed to generate and audit consistent UI.
